---
title: "No nVidia Splash Screen on Boot"
date: 2011-02-05
forum: New to Ubuntu
---

### Post by SKnudson on 2011-02-05
Hi,

I'm using Ubuntu 10.10 64 bit with a GTS 250 GPU and don't see the nVidia splash screen logo on boot. I'm using the 270.18 driver (NVIDIA accelerated graphics driver (version current)) and it's activated/currently in use according to Additional Drivers in System>Administration.

I've tried adding Option "NoLogo" "False" under device into xorg.conf with no success. Current settings:

```
# nvidia-settings: X configuration file generated by nvidia-settings
# nvidia-settings:  version 270.18  (buildd@king)  Sun Jan 23 21:54:00 UTC 2011

Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier     "Layout0"
    Screen      0  "Screen0" 0 0
    InputDevice    "Keyboard0" "CoreKeyboard"
    InputDevice    "Mouse0" "CorePointer"
    Option         "Xinerama" "0"
EndSection

Section "Files"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Mouse0"
    Driver         "mouse"
    Option         "Protocol" "auto"
    Option         "Device" "/dev/psaux"
    Option         "Emulate3Buttons" "no"
    Option         "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    # generated from default
    Identifier     "Keyboard0"
    Driver         "kbd"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    # HorizSync source: edid, VertRefresh source: edid
    Identifier     "Monitor0"
    VendorName     "Unknown"
    ModelName      "ViewSonic VA2231 Series"
    HorizSync       24.0 - 82.0
    VertRefresh     50.0 - 75.0
    Option         "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier     "Device0"
    Driver         "nvidia"
    VendorName     "NVIDIA Corporation"
    BoardName      "GeForce GTS 250"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier     "Screen0"
    Device         "Device0"
    Monitor        "Monitor0"
    DefaultDepth    24
    Option         "TwinView" "0"
    Option         "metamodes" "1920x1080_60 +0+0; nvidia-auto-select +0+0"
    SubSection     "Display"
        Depth       24
    EndSubSection
EndSection

```

I also tried changing quiet splash to splash in /etc/default/grub without any success. 

This is a pretty fresh installation and my first Linux experience, so I could very well be missing packages if they're needed. The driver is otherwise behaving as it should as far as I can tell (variable fan setting automatically adjusts to load, thermal sensors reading temps, resolution changes from X Server Settings work), but the added confirmation would be appreciated :).

Thanks

### Post by cwsnyder on 2011-02-05
OK. I think I know what you are talking about, but I haven't _ever_ had a graphics card splash on a Linux system.  Only on Windows.
